procedure.requested_by: Dr. Robert Chen, MD — Spine Surgery

denial.reason_text: Request denied based on peer-to-peer review. Plan medical director spoke with requesting physician on 10/12/2024. Based on that discussion, clinical criteria for surgery not met. Detailed criteria review provided verbally during peer-to-peer call.

appeal_basis: Denial criteria communicated verbally only — member never received written criteria
CHSC 1374.31 requires written criteria in denial notice
Verbal peer-to-peer cannot substitute for written denial notice

mri_1.findings: C6-C7 disc herniation, right C7 nerve root compression, moderate foraminal stenosis

neurological_exam.findings: Right C7 dermatomal numbness and tingling, tricep reflex diminished, grip 4+/5

physical_therapy_summary.note: 5 weeks PT. Plateau not formally documented but treating physician declares plateau in appeal letter.

question: Should the prior authorization denial for ACDF C6-C7 (CPT 22551) be overturned on appeal?

ground_truth_complexity.obvious_reading: UPHOLD — 5 weeks PT (need 6), no injection, no formal plateau documentation. Clinical criteria shortfall.
ground_truth_complexity.harder_question_1: Does a verbal peer-to-peer communication of denial criteria satisfy CHSC 1374.31(b) written notice requirement?
ground_truth_complexity.harder_question_2: Can the plan rely on what was said in a peer-to-peer call as the basis for denial without documenting those criteria in writing?
ground_truth_complexity.harder_question_3: Is this a REMAND (reissue with proper written notice) or UPHOLD (clinical criteria not met, procedural defect does not change outcome)?
ground_truth_complexity.harder_question_4: If the written criteria requirement is violated, can the member meaningfully appeal without knowing exactly what the plan applied?
ground_truth_complexity.the_genuine_tension: Clinical criteria not met on the merits — 5 weeks PT, no injection, plateau not formally documented. The denial is substantively defensible. But the procedural violation (criteria communicated verbally only, no IMR notice) independently requires remedy.
ground_truth_complexity.right_answer: REMAND — The clinical denial may be substantively correct (5 weeks PT vs 6 required, no injection, plateau not formally documented in PT records). However, CHSC 1374.31(b) requires written criteria in the denial notice. Verbal peer-to-peer communication cannot substitute for written notice. Plan must reissue a compliant written denial.
